name: Molino del Pasadero BTT Trail
name_original: Trabanca - Villarino de los Aires – circular por el Molino de las Pasaderas y la Ruta del Duero
description: Experience the Molino del Pasadero BTT Trail, a difficult 18.4-mile mountain biking route with river views in Parque Natural de Arribes del
region_intro: Mountain biking around Trabanca offers diverse terrain at the foothills of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park. The region is characterized by a mix of granite and shale formations, providing a dynamic landscape for riders. Mountain bikers can explore verdant valleys and discover various water features, adding to the scenic appeal of the trails. This challenging topography includes both technical descents and climbs, catering to different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Trabanca The most popular mountain bike trail is Duero Trail, a 13.1 miles (21.1 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete, offering scenic views along the Duero river. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Los cañones del Tormes y presa de Almendra — circular desde Fermoselle, a difficult 23.4 miles (37.7 km) path. This route navigates through canyons and past the Almendra dam, providing a challenging circular experience. Local mountain bikers also love the Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle, a 36.3 miles (58.4 km) trail leading through the Sayago region, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Trabanca is defined by granite and shale formations, lush valleys, and challenging topography. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult mountain bike trails. The routes in Trabanca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from a few reviews. More than 20 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Trabanca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Trabanca?
A: Trabanca offers a selection of 11 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and difficult routes to explore the region's diverse terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Trabanca?
A: The terrain around Trabanca is characterized by a dynamic mix of granite and shale formations. Riders can expect to navigate through verdant valleys and encounter various water features, providing a challenging and scenic experience at the foothills of the Serra da Estrela Natural Park.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Trabanca?
A: The region is particularly pleasant for mountain biking when wildflowers are in bloom, enhancing the natural beauty of the trails. Generally, the weather during these periods is ideal for outdoor activities, offering comfortable conditions for riding.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders in Trabanca?
A: Yes, Trabanca offers several challenging routes for experienced mountain bikers. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018268987/los-canones-del-tormes-y-presa-de-almendra-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los cañones del Tormes y presa de Almendra — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 37.7 km (23.4 miles) path that navigates through canyons and past the Almendra dam. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt;, a 58.4 km (36.3 miles) trail through the Sayago region.

Q: What scenic points or landmarks can I see along mountain bike trails in Trabanca?
A: Mountain bike trails in Trabanca offer access to several scenic points and natural landmarks. You can enjoy panoramic views from various viewpoints like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4791099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Staircase Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437985" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña del Cura Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4899801" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3539630" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almendra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6065885" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salce Beach – Almendra Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are also notable water features you might encounter.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Trabanca?
A: Yes, several mountain bike routes in Trabanca are circular. Examples include the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018268987/los-canones-del-tormes-y-presa-de-almendra-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los cañones del Tormes y presa de Almendra — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete loop experience.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Trabanca?
A: The mountain bike trails in Trabanca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Riders often praise the region's varied terrain, which includes granite and shale formations, lush valleys, and challenging topography, offering options for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty mountain bike trails in Trabanca?
A: Yes, there are moderate difficulty mountain bike trails available. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018871162/duero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 21.1 km (13.1 miles) route that offers scenic views along the Duero river and takes approximately 1 hour 56 minutes to complete.

Q: Are there any natural features to explore while mountain biking in Trabanca?
A: Absolutely. Trabanca's mountain biking routes often lead through diverse natural features, including verdant valleys and various water features. The region's unique geological formations of granite and shale also contribute to a visually interesting and varied landscape.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, some trails offer views of or pass near significant water bodies. For example, the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018268987/los-canones-del-tormes-y-presa-de-almendra-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los cañones del Tormes y presa de Almendra — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; route navigates past the Almendra dam and reservoir, providing impressive water views.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on mountain bike trails in Trabanca?
A: The elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Trabanca varies depending on the route. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018871162/duero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Duero Trail&lt;/a&gt; has an elevation gain of approximately 372 meters, while more challenging routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt; can involve over 700 meters of ascent.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails suitable for a full day's ride?
A: Yes, several trails are long enough for a full day's adventure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017750041/ruta-de-los-arrieros-en-sayago-circular-desde-fermoselle" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Arrieros en Sayago — circular desde Fermoselle&lt;/a&gt;, at 58.4 km (36.3 miles), typically takes around 5 hours to complete, making it an excellent option for an extended ride.
tips: The ruins of the old Molino de las Pasaderas mill are located between Trabanca and Villarino de los Aires and you can visit the inside where part of the original machinery still remains.

The La Faya viewpoint, located in the very urban area of Villarino de los Aires, offers us an impressive view of the Duero River and a large part of the Arribes Natural Park, also reaching a large area of neighboring Portugal.
This track of the natural path of the Duero runs through a beautiful landscape with old oak trees and granite masses that peek out of the ground.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain biking trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. The Lecrín Valley region, where this trail is located, features diverse terrain that can include steep sections, rocks, and roots, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. It's designed for experienced mountain bikers.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of terrain, typical of the Lecrín Valley. This includes tracks originally used by farmers, passing through pine forests, olive, almond, and citrus groves. Expect varied surfaces, potentially with steep sections and some technical elements like rocks and roots.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the historical Molino de las Pasaderas, and enjoy panoramic views from the View from Mirador de la Faya. A significant portion of the route also follows the scenic Duero Route.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Approximately 39% of it follows the Camino del Molino del Pasadero, and about 38% is part of the Ruta del Duero. You'll also find it intersects with parts of Etapa 33: Trabanca – Villarino de los Aires and Camino del Teso de San Cristóbal.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts near Trabanca and Villarino de los Aires. While specific parking details for this exact route are not provided, you can typically find parking in or near these towns, which serve as common starting points for routes in the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails within the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero. However, it's always best to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife and agricultural areas. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: The Lecrín Valley and surrounding areas are pleasant for mountain biking in the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring cooler, wetter conditions, though riding is often possible year-round.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required to access the trails within the Parque Natural de Arribes del Duero for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, always respect local regulations and private property signage.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is situated near towns like Trabanca and Villarino de los Aires. These villages, and others in the broader Lecrín Valley, offer local amenities including cafes, restaurants, and various accommodation options, allowing you to refuel and relax after your ride.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while riding?
faqs.answer: You'll experience the scenic beauty characteristic of the Lecrín Valley and the Arribes del Duero. Expect panoramic views, often extending to the coast on clear days, as you pass through charming towns, pine forests, and lush agricultural areas with olive, almond, and citrus groves.

faqs.question: Is there public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: While direct public transport to the exact trailhead might be limited, regional bus services connect larger towns in the area, such as Trabanca and Villarino de los Aires. From these points, you might need to cycle or arrange local transport to the specific starting point of the trail.
